sanskrit: Representative Sanskrit placeholder for 1.2. Replace with exact source text if preparing a licensed edition.
english: This verse belongs to chapter 1 and is grouped under Grief, Confusion, Compassion, Moral Conflict for emotion-based study.

sanskrit: Representative Sanskrit placeholder for 1.3. Replace with exact source text if preparing a licensed edition.
english: This verse belongs to chapter 1 and is grouped under Grief, Confusion, Compassion, Moral Conflict for emotion-based study.
Seeing my own kinsmen arrayed and eager to fight, O Krishna.

sanskrit: sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ ca pariśuṣyati
english: My limbs fail, my mouth dries up, and my body trembles.

sanskrit: vepathuś ca śarīre me roma-harṣaś ca jāyate
english: My hair stands on end, my bow slips, and my mind reels.
My nature is overcome by weakness and confusion about duty; I ask You to guide me.

sanskrit: aśocyān anvaśocas tvaṁ prajñā-vādāṁś ca bhāṣase
english: You grieve for what is not worthy of grief, yet speak words of wisdom.
Pleasure and pain come and go through sense contact; learn to endure them.

sanskrit: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana
english: You have a right to action, but never to the fruits of action.
One who is not disturbed in distress and not attached in happiness is steady in wisdom.
Dwelling on sense objects develops attachment.
From attachment comes desire, from desire anger, from anger delusion.

sanskrit: āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat
english: Peace comes to one into whom desires enter like rivers into a full, still ocean.

sanskrit: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara
english: Therefore perform your duty without attachment.
Surrender all actions to Me with mind centered in the self.

sanskrit: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
english: Whenever righteousness declines, I manifest Myself.
I appear to protect the good, destroy evil, and restore dharma.
A faithful person who controls the senses gains knowledge.
The wise see with equal vision a learned person, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste.
Peace comes from knowing Me as the enjoyer, Lord, and friend of all beings.
Let a person lift the self by the self and not degrade the self.
The mind is friend to the disciplined and enemy to the uncontrolled.

sanskrit: yato yato niścarati manaś cañcalam asthiram
english: Wherever the restless mind wanders, bring it back under the control of the self.
The mind is restless, turbulent, strong, and stubborn.

sanskrit: daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā
english: My divine energy is hard to overcome, but those who surrender to Me cross beyond it.

sanskrit: bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate
english: After many births, the wise surrender, knowing that all is rooted in the Divine.

sanskrit: tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu mām anusmara yudhya ca
english: Remember Me at all times and still do your duty.
I protect what My devoted followers have and provide what they lack.

sanskrit: patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati
english: If one offers Me a leaf, flower, fruit, or water with devotion, I accept it.
I am equal to all beings, yet I am especially near to My devotee.
Even one of poor conduct, if devoted exclusively, should be regarded as saintly.
Think of Me, become devoted to Me, worship Me, and offer your homage to Me.
I am the source of all; from Me everything proceeds.
To those devoted with love, I give understanding by which they come to Me.
Out of compassion, I destroy the darkness of ignorance with the lamp of knowledge.
If a thousand suns rose at once in the sky, their brilliance might resemble that form.
Seeing Your vast form with many mouths and eyes, worlds tremble and so do I.
Seeing Your blazing, sky-touching form, my inner self is shaken.
Tell me who You are in this fierce form.
Therefore I bow down and seek Your grace.

sanskrit: adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca
english: One who is free from hatred, friendly, and compassionate is dear to Me.
One from whom the world does not suffer, and who is not disturbed by the world, is dear to Me.
One who is equal toward friend and foe is steady.
Humility, sincerity, nonviolence, tolerance, and simplicity are knowledge.
Seeing the suffering in birth, death, old age, and disease is part of wisdom.
One who serves Me with unfailing devotion rises beyond the modes of nature.
The living being in this world is My eternal fragment.
I am seated in every heart; from Me come memory, knowledge, and forgetfulness.
Fearlessness, purity, self-control, and charity begin divine qualities.
Nonviolence, truthfulness, and freedom from anger mark divine character.
Lust, anger, and greed are the three gates leading to ruin.
A person is shaped by faith; whatever one’s faith is, that one becomes.

sanskrit: anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitaṁ ca yat
english: Speech that does not agitate, is truthful, pleasing, and beneficial is discipline of speech.
With mind fixed on Me, you will overcome all difficulties by My grace.
The Lord dwells in the heart of all beings and directs their journey.
Fix your mind on Me, be My devotee, worship Me, and bow to Me.
Abandon all other duties and take refuge in Me alone.
My delusion is destroyed, memory restored, and I am ready to act.
Where Krishna and Arjuna stand together, there are victory, prosperity, and righteousness.
